afterFileEdit.prompt: An edit just finished. If the edited file is documentation (e.g. .md) or a schema/enum/config/JSON that other docs or assets depend on, gently note that a sync check via the document-review-sync-check skill (L1 reference existence / L2 parameter consistency / L3 reverse propagation) may be warranted before treating the change as complete. This is advisory only: do NOT block, do NOT auto-run a full review, keep it to a one-line reminder. If the file is unrelated, stay silent. Hook input: $ARGUMENTS

beforeShellExecution.prompt:
You are the unified-review-suite pre-commit gate. A shell command is about to run. Act ONLY if it creates a git commit (e.g. matches `git commit`); for anything else return {"permission":"allow"}.

For a commit, enforce a BLOCKING review checkpoint:
1. Decide whether a unified review (the review-orchestrator skill or the /review command) has ALREADY been completed in THIS session covering the changes being committed, AND that no unresolved P0 (blocking) or P1 (important) issues remain.
2. If a clean review was completed (no open P0/P1): return {"permission":"allow"}.
3. Otherwise — no review was done, unresolved P0/P1 remain, or you cannot confirm it — return {"permission":"deny"} with:
   - user_message: tell the user the commit was blocked by the unified-review-suite gate; run /review (or the review-orchestrator skill), resolve all P0/P1 issues, then commit again. Mention they can bypass by committing in an external terminal if truly necessary.
   - agent_message: instruct the agent to run /review now, fix every P0/P1 issue, then retry the commit.

Default to deny when uncertain — this gate is intentionally aggressive to prevent committing unreviewed changes. Hook input: $ARGUMENTS
